title: dichanthelium wilcoxianum
intro: Dichanthelium wilcoxianum, also known as Wilcox's rosette grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to the United States and is often found growing in dry to moist meadows and open woods. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dichanthelium wilcoxianum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, and it can be prepared for medicinal use through teas, infusions, and tinctures.
